edibility: Inedible — toxicity unverified; many Entoloma species are toxic, so it is never eaten. The Midnight Blue Entoloma should be treated as potentially toxic given the genus's track record.
taste: Not consumed due to inedible status.
search: Found in autumn–winter in damp New Zealand native forests on mossy ground. Identification clues: deep blue coloration throughout, conical cap shape. Look-alikes include other blue Entoloma species, though the distinctive coloration and cap shape help narrow identification.

toxicity.symptoms: Toxicity unverified — best treated as potentially toxic given Entoloma genus; many species cause gastrointestinal distress
toxicity.onset: Unknown; many Entoloma species cause gastrointestinal symptoms within hours of ingestion
toxicity.treatmentWindow: Early medical intervention within 2–4 hours of ingestion is recommended if toxin exposure is suspected
toxicity.antidote: Supportive care and activated charcoal if eaten; medical attention should be sought immediately
